reasoning: The yfinance tool provided a combined statistical estimate of 0.44 for the near-term probability of an increase, reflecting seasonal downward pressure compared to prior years. Since short-term stock movements are highly unpredictable, I anchor closely on this statistical baseline. For the first resolution date (Nov 30), I estimate a 0.44 probability of an increase relative to the Nov 23 baseline. As the time horizon increases to Dec 23 and Feb 21, uncertainty increases, pulling the estimates closer to 0.5. Additionally, FRT traditionally goes ex-dividend in late December (e.g., Dec 29-31) and pays a relatively high quarterly dividend of ~$1.13. This mechanical drop in stock price falls between the Dec 23 and Feb 21 resolution dates, offsetting the natural upward drift in stock prices over a 3-month horizon and keeping the Feb 21 probability slightly below 0.5. Conforming to the rule that uncertainty must increase with horizon, the final probabilities are 0.44, 0.47, and 0.49.
